name: Shortbread Sheep
recipeInstructions:
Preparation time: 25 minutes Cooking time: 20 to 25 minutes
1. Cream together the butter and sugar in a large mixing bowl until light and fluffy. Beat in the flour and salt. Knead the dough briefly until smooth. If the dough is too sticky, add a bit more flour. (The dough can be refrigerated for several days; let it soften slightly before shaping cookies.)
2. Preheat oven to 325 degrees Fahrenheit. Have ungreased baking sheets ready.
3. Roll out half of the dough on a lightly floured surface to a thickness of about 1/2 inch. Use a sheep cookie cutter to cut out desired shapes. Place the cookies about 2 inches apart on the prepared baking sheets. Bake until the edges are light brown, approximately 20 to 25 minutes. Cool on wire racks.
Note: The sheep cookie cutter used by Betty J. Koenig was a Hallmark cutter that is no longer available. A similar shape can be made using a piece of cardboard placed over the dough and cut out with a small knife. For an added touch, use fingertips to create dimples in the dough to resemble wool. Optionally, place a small piece of chocolate on top for eyes after baking.
